title: hieracium cirritum subsp. subtenellum
benefits: Hieracium cirritum subsp. subtenellum has many benefits, such as being a rich source of antioxidants, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases like cancer and heart disease. This plant also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate symptoms of arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory conditions, improving overall joint health and mobility. Additionally, Hieracium cirritum subsp. subtenellum has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections and promote wound healing, while its antiseptic properties can soothe skin irritations and promote healthy skin. By harnessing the benefits of this plant, individuals can enjoy improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of disease, ultimately leading to a longer and more fulfilling life.
constituents: Hieracium cirritum subsp. subtenellum has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin), phenolic acids (chlorogenic acid and ferulic acid), terpenoids (betulinic acid and ursolic acid), and phenylethanoid glycosides (acetoside and verbascoside). These compounds exhibit a range of beneficial effects, including anti-inflammatory properties, antioxidant activity, and antimicrobial action, which can improve daily life by reducing the risk of chronic diseases, promoting wound healing, and enhancing immune function. The flavonoids present in Hieracium cirritum subsp. subtenellum, for instance, have been shown to exhibit anti-cancer properties and improve cardiovascular health, while the terpenoids have been found to possess anti-viral and anti-bacterial properties, making them useful in the treatment of various infections. By harnessing the medicinal properties of Hieracium cirritum subsp. subtenellum, individuals can potentially enjoy improved overall health and well-being, leading to a better quality of life.
parts: Hieracium cirritum subsp. subtenellum has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, flowers, and roots, which contain various bioactive compounds. The leaves of this plant are known to contain flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of Hieracium cirritum subsp. subtenellum contain sesquiterpene lactones, such as eremophyllin C, which have been studied for their potential antimicrobial and anti-cancer activities. The roots of this plant are rich in triterpenoid saponins, including helenin and alantolactone, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.
side_effects: Hieracium cirritum subsp. subtenellum can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals. Prolonged consumption of the plant may lead to liver damage and kidney problems due to the presence of toxic compounds. In severe cases, misuse of Hieracium cirritum subsp. subtenellum can cause vomiting, dizziness, and even seizures, emphasizing the need for caution and proper guidance. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
